rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
What are the big issues affecting the world today? This course is structured into three units that each focus on one important global issue. In order to understand these global issues, each unit also presents a thinking strategy. The global issues and thinking strategies covered in the three units are:
1) Climate change (Critical thinking)
2) Plastic pollution (Micro/meso/macro analysis)
3) Seeing both sides of the story* (Objectivity)
Each unit will follow a similar structure of four lessons:
Lesson 1) Introduces the topic and explains why it is a problem. Students will discuss the reading and comprehension activities to gain a contextual background of the global issue.
Lesson 2) Introduces the thinking strategy through which we will consider the issue and the presentation task. Students will complete their presentation plan for homework.
Lesson 3) A range of relevant articles related to the issue are introduced. Students submit their presentation plan and the teacher will provide feedback and direct them to relevant articles during class. Students will produce a summary of one of the articles and prepare their presentation for homework.
Lesson 4) Students give their presentation.
*The “seeing both sides of the story” unit will look at a contemporary event and will culminate in a discussion activity. The event that we will be doing will be announced in class.
aims:
>Consider different large-scale issues which are affecting global society at present
>Utilize different ways of thinking to analyze global issues
>Relate your opinions and ideas using speaking and writing
goals: Learn about the current events in our world.
Take a global perspective (look at events in other countries -- make comparisons)

plan.text:
Class introduction
Introduce the important information for the course and take an introductory quiz about global issues.

Academic referencing
Students will learn academic referencing following the APA style. This is an important skill that will be used in the presentations and final assessment task.

Climate change 1
The teacher will outline the issue for this unit. Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates

Climate change 2
Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates. The teacher will explain the assessment task and associated thinking strategy.

Climate change 3
Students will get feedback on their assessment plan and will check the articles/case studies presented by the teacher.

Plastic pollution 1
The teacher will outline the issue for this unit. Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates

Plastic pollution 2
The teacher will outline the issue for this unit. Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates

Plastic pollution 3
Students will get feedback on their assessment plan and will check the articles/case studies presented by the teacher.

Seeing both sides of the story 1
The teacher will outline the issue for this unit. Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates

Seeing both sides of the story 2
Students will discuss the comprehension questions with classmates. The teacher will explain the conversation task and associated thinking strategy.

Seeing both sides of the story 3
Students will get feedback on their conversation plan and will check the articles/case studies presented by the teacher.

Seeing both sides of the story 4
Students will discuss with classmates

Course overview and final assessment preparation
This class will provide a comparative review of the issues and thinking strategies introduced in this course. It will also broadly introduce the final writing task and provide a preparation activity for this.

textbooks: All materials will be provided in class
prereq:
-Students must not be absent for more than 1/3 of total classes.
-Students must complete all assessments to pass the course.
-Students should be willing to consider different points of view and ways of thinking.
-Students should be willing to participate in class activities including spoken activities and presentations.
prep: It is necessary to complete the preview activities assigned for homework before the following class. Students will be assigned a score for this via the “Participation” mark.
teachers: ◎ マガシック ミケランジェロ

履修に係わる注意事項
【NOTES ON CLASS ENROLLMENT】
extra.text: Global issues presented here in the curriculum are a general guide. There may be some variation in the issues presented from year to year.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
Participation- 30% ( The participation score will be calculated based on participation in class discussions, blog posts, and activeness in class. Information about this will be provided during Lesson 1.)
Final task - 20% (The final task will consist of two short essay questions (2 x 250 words = approx. 500 words), which are completed during class time.)
Presentation 1 - 25%; Presentation 2 - 25% (3-min. presentation in English -- practiced speaking (not reading) is important)
Presentations will be completed using PowerPoint software.
Generally speaking, presentations and the final test will be scored according to the following criteria: language 50%, depth 50%. “Language” means answering the question, complying with word/time limits, using effective vocabulary and structure, and overall intelligibility; and “depth” means knowledge of lesson content, using examples and references, taking a global perspective and overall sophistication of argument. Specific criteria will be provided in class.
